africaFirstNotes: Buka model found across West Africa; Jollof rice debates cross-border
audienceSummary: Nigerians searching for a local eatery — families, office workers, students. They want to know the menu, location, and how to order (WhatsApp or walk-in).
businessContextSummary: Nigerian restaurants range from roadside bukas to semi-formal eateries. Most operate on daily menus. WhatsApp ordering is standard. Trust is built through food photos, customer names, and local reputation.
contentLocalizationNotes: Use 'Eatery' and 'Buka' alongside 'Restaurant'. Describe dishes by their Nigerian names (jollof rice, egusi soup, puff-puff, shawarma). Use 'Order via WhatsApp' as primary CTA. Reference opening from 7am for breakfast-serving spots.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian food photography: jollof rice served on Nigerian-brand plates, suya on skewers, a Nigerian waiter or cook smiling, busy Lagos eatery interior. No generic white-background restaurant stock images.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: NAFDAC compliance for food businesses; CAC registration number for formal eateries. Hygiene rating or association membership if applicable.
nextAction: Claim niche and begin 4-thread parallel research on Nigerian restaurant website patterns
notes: First niche in queue. Serves as reference implementation for all commerce food verticals.

africaFirstNotes: Pan-African political campaign patterns apply; Afrobeats cultural references in campaign language
audienceSummary: Nigerian voters and constituents — ward, LGA, state, or federal levels. They want to know the candidate's background, manifesto, and contact details.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian politicians need campaign sites for elections from Councillor to President (7 office types). Sites must emphasise party affiliation, ward/constituency coverage, and constituent contact.
contentLocalizationNotes: Use Nigerian political language: 'constituent', 'manifesto', 'ward', 'senatorial district', 'governorship'. Reference INEC voter registration. Party colours and symbols are central to design.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian politician in traditional attire or formal suit at rally. Nigerian community meetings. Constituency development photos. Party flag and colours.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: INEC registration acknowledgement. Party card / membership number. CAC for party organisations.
nextAction: Research Nigerian political campaign website patterns and constituent engagement models
notes: M8b milestone. 7 office types from Councillor to President. Party affiliation is central design element.

audienceSummary: Party members, prospective members, journalists, and Nigerian voters seeking party information, manifesto, and candidate listings.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian political parties (APC, PDP, LP, etc.) need organisation websites with membership, manifesto, news, and candidate profiles. Must handle party structure from national to ward level.
contentLocalizationNotes: Use official party language and structure. Nigerian political parties have Zonal, State, LGA, Ward chapters. Reference INEC party registration.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Party rally in Nigerian stadium or town hall. Party colours throughout. Flagbearers and candidates in party uniform.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: INEC party registration certificate. Party CAC registration.
nextAction: Research Nigerian political party website structures and INEC compliance requirements
notes: M8b milestone. Organisation entity type. Inherits from politician pattern but organisation-scoped.

africaFirstNotes: Ride-hailing strong in Lagos, Abuja, Accra, Nairobi — pan-African pattern
audienceSummary: Nigerians needing rides and drivers seeking to join a network. Lagos, Abuja, and other major cities.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian ride-hailing operates against Bolt, Uber, and informal okada/keke networks. FRSC compliance and driver verification are trust signals. WhatsApp dispatch is common for smaller operators.
contentLocalizationNotes: Emphasise FRSC-verified drivers, safety record, coverage areas (by city/LGA). Use 'booking' rather than 'ride request'.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian drivers with verified badges in front of car. Lagos/Abuja traffic or highway. Smiling Nigerian passenger in backseat.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: FRSC driver licence verification. VIO vehicle roadworthiness. Driver KYC documentation.
nextAction: Research Nigerian ride-hailing operator website patterns and FRSC compliance display
notes: M8c milestone. FRSC verification is central trust signal.

africaFirstNotes: Cross-border haulage Lagos-Cotonou-Accra-Abidjan is major business corridor
audienceSummary: Nigerian manufacturers, traders, and importers needing goods moved. B2B audience searching for fleet size, route coverage, and reliability track record.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian haulage companies move goods by road. FRSC fleet certification and CAC registration are the trust foundations. Route coverage across Nigeria's 6 geopolitical zones.
contentLocalizationNotes: Emphasise fleet size (number of trucks), covered routes (Lagos-Kano, Lagos-Abuja, Port Harcourt-Aba), FRSC verification, and CAC registration.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian articulated truck on expressway with Nigerian number plate. Loading dock at Lagos port. Driver with FRSC certificate.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: FRSC fleet certification. CAC Business registration. Goods-in-transit insurance. SON weight compliance.
nextAction: Research Nigerian haulage company website patterns and B2B logistics trust signals
notes: M8c milestone. B2B audience. Fleet size and route coverage are key content areas.

africaFirstNotes: Nigerian church model (Pentecostal, Anglican, Catholic, Redeemed) is Africa's most developed; templates should serve all denominations
audienceSummary: Church members, prospective members, and Nigerians searching for a church in their area. They want service times, location, sermon access, and how to give.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian churches are among the world's largest organisations. From local assemblies to megachurches (RCCG, Winners, MFM). IT-registration required. Community platform is central. Online giving is growing rapidly.
contentLocalizationNotes: Use 'Service Times' not 'Mass Schedule' (unless Catholic). 'Giving' not 'Donations'. 'Pastor' or 'G.O.' (General Overseer) as leadership title. 'Branch' for locations. WhatsApp for visitor follow-up.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian congregation in praise session. Contemporary Nigerian church building exterior. Pastor preaching to crowd. Youth fellowship group of young Nigerians.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: IT-XXXXXXXX (Incorporated Trustees) registration number. CAC registration for mega-churches. SCOAN / RCCG / other denomination affiliation where applicable.
nextAction: Research Nigerian church website patterns across denominations and online giving models
notes: M8d milestone. IT-registration required. Must serve both local assembly and megachurch scale. Community platform integration.

africaFirstNotes: Development NGOs operate across West Africa; donor reports often required in international formats
audienceSummary: Nigerian donors, volunteers, partner organisations, and international funders. They want impact evidence, financial transparency, and easy donation pathways.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian NGOs range from grassroots CBO to USAID/DFID-funded INGOs. IT-registration required. Donor trust is built through annual reports, audit certifications, and beneficiary stories.
contentLocalizationNotes: Use 'Donate' and 'Support Us'. Reference Nigerian impact metrics (communities reached by LGA, beneficiaries by state). NDPR consent on any form.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian beneficiaries — women, youth, farmers — in their communities. NGO staff in branded T-shirts at field site. Nigerian children in school setting for education NGOs.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: IT-XXXXXXXX (Incorporated Trustees). CAC registration. Audit certification. Partnership logos (UN, USAID, state government).
nextAction: Research Nigerian NGO website patterns and donor trust signal models
notes: M8d milestone. IT-registration is central trust signal. Impact reporting is a major content area.

audienceSummary: Nigerian business owners operating a POS terminal, small retail, or service business. The 'website' is primarily an operations portal/back-office, not a consumer-facing site.
businessContextSummary: POS Business is the Nigerian informal/semi-formal SME category. Pillar 2 here is an operations portal — not a customer-facing website. It shows business performance, inventory, and staff management dashboards.
contentLocalizationNotes: Portal language: 'Today's Sales', 'Stock Levels', 'Staff On Duty'. Use Nigerian Naira throughout. Reference common Nigerian business tools (accounting, payroll).
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian shopkeeper at POS terminal. Nigerian market stall with stock display. Business owner with tablet reviewing sales data.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: CAC Business Name registration. FIRS TIN. State Board of Internal Revenue compliance.
nextAction: Research Nigerian POS business management portal UX patterns and key operational metrics displayed
notes: M8b milestone. ops+branding only (no marketplace). This is an OPERATIONS PORTAL not a consumer website. Distinct from POS agent infrastructure.

africaFirstNotes: Informal artisan economy dominant across sub-Saharan Africa
audienceSummary: Nigerians seeking a local tradesperson or artisan — by neighbourhood or LGA. WhatsApp catalogue is the primary contact method.
businessContextSummary: Nigeria's informal economy is dominated by sole traders — tailors, cobblers, welders, plumbers, carpenters. A Pillar 2 site is their first digital shop window. WhatsApp catalogue integration is the killer feature.
contentLocalizationNotes: Use informal, direct Nigerian language. 'My Work', 'What I Do', 'Call Me', 'WhatsApp Me'. Reference area of operation by Lagos area / Abuja zone / any Nigerian city neighbourhood.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian artisan at work — tailor at sewing machine, carpenter with timber, plumber fixing pipe. Real workshop setting, not studio. Genuine hands-on craft.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: Business Name registration is optional for sole traders but encouraged. Vocational training certificate if applicable.
nextAction: Research Nigerian sole trader digital presence patterns and WhatsApp catalogue integration design
notes: M8e milestone. ops+branding only. WhatsApp catalogue integration is the central Pillar 2 feature for this vertical.

africaFirstNotes: Professional licensing bodies (NBA, NMA, ICAN) are pan-African recognition signals
audienceSummary: Nigerians seeking a licensed professional — lawyer, doctor, accountant, architect, surveyor. They want to verify credentials, understand specialisations, and book consultations.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian licensed professionals must display their professional body membership. NBA for lawyers, NMA for doctors, ICAN for accountants. Appointment booking via WhatsApp or phone is standard.
contentLocalizationNotes: Use formal Nigerian professional language. 'Specialisation' not 'specialty'. 'Chambers' for law, 'Practice' for medicine and accounting. License number prominently displayed.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian professional in formal attire at office desk. Law chambers with bookshelf of Nigerian statutes. Doctor in white coat at Nigerian clinic.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: Professional body membership number (NBA, NMA, ICAN, NIA, SURCON). Practice license issued by regulatory body.
nextAction: Research Nigerian licensed professional website patterns across law, medicine, accounting
notes: M8e milestone. Individual entity type. Generic professional template — serves as base for law-firm, tax-consultant, and other specific professional niches.

africaFirstNotes: Nigerian school model (private school fees, uniform requirements, terminal exams) widely replicated across West Africa
audienceSummary: Nigerian parents seeking schools for their children. They want to see curriculum, school fees (in Naira), facilities, WAEC/NECO track record, and admission requirements.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian private schools from nursery to secondary. School fees are a major trust barrier — publishing fee schedules builds trust. WAEC/NECO results, state Ministry approval, and WASCE are key trust signals.
contentLocalizationNotes: Use Nigerian school terminology: 'term', 'JSS1-SS3', 'WAEC', 'NECO', 'school fees per term', 'admission form'. Reference Nigerian school calendar. 'Proprietor' or 'Proprietress' as school owner title.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian students in school uniforms. Classroom with Nigerian textbooks. School building with compound gate. Nigerian teachers at board.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: State Ministry of Education approval. WAEC centre accreditation. CAC registration. School uniform and official seal.
nextAction: Research Nigerian private school website patterns — admission, fees, results display
notes: M8e milestone. Community_courses module reuse. Covers nursery through secondary school.

africaFirstNotes: Primary care access patterns common across West Africa; malaria, typhoid, hypertension are common presenting conditions
audienceSummary: Nigerians seeking a reliable local clinic — for general consultation, antenatal care, or specialised services. They want to know opening hours, doctors' names, and what conditions are treated.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian clinics range from one-doctor GP practices to polyclinics. MDCN licence and NAFDAC compliance are mandatory. Appointment booking via WhatsApp is standard. HMO acceptance is a key trust signal.
contentLocalizationNotes: Use 'Consultation', 'Antenatal', 'HMO Accepted', 'NHIS'. Reference common Nigerian health concerns (malaria testing, hypertension management). 'Book an Appointment' via WhatsApp.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian doctor in white coat with patient. Clinic reception with Nigerian nurse. Waiting area with Nigerian health posters.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: MDCN (Medical and Dental Council of Nigeria) licence. NAFDAC compliance. CAC registration. HMO partnerships. NHIS accreditation.
nextAction: Research Nigerian primary care clinic website patterns and MDCN/HMO trust signal display
notes: M8e milestone. License verification (MDCN/NAFDAC) is the central compliance feature.

africaFirstNotes: Afrobeats, Nollywood, and Nigerian fashion creators have global audiences — Africa-first content has worldwide reach
audienceSummary: Fans, brand partners, event promoters, and media professionals seeking to book or collaborate with a Nigerian creator/influencer.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian creators span YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, Twitter (X), and podcasting. Their website is a media kit + booking page + content hub. Brand deals are primary monetisation.
contentLocalizationNotes: Use creator economy language: 'Collaboration', 'Brand Deals', 'Content Creator', 'Influencer'. Reference Nigerian platforms and content: Naija pop culture, Afrobeats, pidgin content.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian creator recording content — YouTube studio setup in Lagos apartment. Nigerian influencer at brand event. Nollywood-aesthetic photo shoot.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: NBMC content code compliance for broadcast creators. Copyright registration for music creators.
nextAction: Research Nigerian creator/influencer personal brand website patterns and brand deal showcase formats
notes: M8e milestone. Social network + community as creator tools. Paid subscription tiers.

africaFirstNotes: Yaba tech corridor, Andela, Flutterwave, and other Nigerian tech hubs have pan-African reputation
audienceSummary: Startups seeking coworking space, investors looking for deal flow, developers and students seeking community, and programme applicants.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian tech hubs (CcHUB, FATE Foundation, The Venture Garden, etc.) provide coworking, incubation, and investor access. They serve a sophisticated tech audience comfortable with English-language content.
contentLocalizationNotes: Use startup/tech language but ground it in Nigeria: 'Naija tech ecosystem', 'Lagos tech scene', 'Apply for our cohort'. Reference Nigeria's startup milestones (Paystack, Flutterwave exits).
imageArtDirectionNotes: Young Nigerian developers on laptops in open-plan hub. Pitch day presentation at Lagos tech event. CcHUB-style building exterior. Afrotech community vibe.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: CAC registration. Any government or international accelerator affiliation (NITDA, GIZ, World Bank). Listed portfolio companies as trust signals.
nextAction: Research Nigerian tech hub website patterns and incubator programme presentation
notes: M8e milestone. Place entity type. Community module integration.

audienceSummary: Nigerians and business travellers booking accommodation. Shortlet bookers in Lagos / Abuja. They want room photos, pricing, availability, and WhatsApp booking.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian hotels and shortlets compete with Airbnb and Booking.com. The local advantage is WhatsApp booking, naira pricing, and locality trust. Caution: power supply (24h generator) is a unique Nigerian trust signal.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature '24-hour power supply', 'Wi-Fi', 'CCTV', 'Secure parking'. Price in NGN per night. Reference airport proximity (for Abuja/Lagos airport hotels). 'Book now via WhatsApp'.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian hotel room — clean, well-lit, Nigerian décor. Hotel pool or lounge area. Hotel exterior with Nigerian-style façade. Security gate.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: CAC registration. State Tourism Board / Hotel licensing. NAFDAC food handling for in-house restaurant.
nextAction: Research Nigerian hotel and shortlet website patterns and booking trust signals
notes: M9 milestone. Room inventory and booking calendar are core features. Generator power is a unique Nigerian trust signal.

audienceSummary: Nigerian household shoppers and delivery app users. They want product availability, delivery options, and WhatsApp ordering.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian supermarkets compete with Shoprite, SPAR, and local open markets. Trust is built through product availability, clean store imagery, and WhatsApp ordering convenience.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Home Delivery to Lagos Island/Mainland', 'Order via WhatsApp', daily specials in NGN. Reference Nigerian staple products (garri, semolina, palm oil, Golden Morn).
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian supermarket aisle with Nigerian products. Cashier at Nigerian POS terminal. Home delivery box with Nigerian address label.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: NAFDAC food handling compliance. SON standards compliance for packaged goods. CAC registration.
nextAction: Research Nigerian supermarket website patterns and product catalogue display
notes: M9 milestone. Product catalog and stock management focus.

audienceSummary: Nigerians purchasing medications, checking drug availability, or asking pharmacist questions. PCN registration is the primary trust signal.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian pharmacies are regulated by PCN (Pharmacists Council of Nigeria). NAFDAC number must display on drug adverts. Counterfeit drug awareness is high — PCN verification is a major trust builder.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'PCN Registered', 'NAFDAC Approved Products', 'Genuine Medications'. Reference generic drug names and popular Nigerian brands. 'Order via WhatsApp' for medication refills.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian pharmacist in white coat behind pharmacy counter. Pharmacy shelves with Nigerian drug brands. PCN certificate on wall.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: PCN registration number (prominent display). NAFDAC compliance. CAC registration. Superintending Pharmacist licence.
nextAction: Research Nigerian pharmacy website patterns and PCN/NAFDAC trust signal display
notes: M9 milestone. NAFDAC verification is a legal requirement for drug advertising. Counterfeit awareness is high.

africaFirstNotes: Nigerian hair and beauty style is globally influential — Afro textures, braiding, gele tying
audienceSummary: Nigerian women and men seeking hair, beauty, or grooming services. Instagram is primary discovery channel; website confirms booking details.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian beauty salons specialise in natural African hair — braiding, relaxers, locs, geles, and makeup. Services in NGN. Instagram portfolio is the standard. WhatsApp booking is universal.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature services in Nigerian context: 'braids', 'weave', 'gele', 'lashes', 'microblading'. Price in NGN. Book via WhatsApp. Stylist portfolio photos.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian woman with beautifully braided hair. Salon interior with styling chairs and Nigerian-brand products. Gele tying. Makeup transformation.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: No specific national licence, but state Environmental Health compliance may apply. Professional beauty training certificate.
nextAction: Research Nigerian beauty salon Instagram-to-website conversion patterns
notes: M9 milestone. Instagram portfolio → website booking is the conversion pattern to design around.

audienceSummary: Nigerians ordering celebration cakes, pastries, or bread for events. WhatsApp ordering for custom orders.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian bakeries produce custom celebration cakes (birthday, wedding, naming ceremony), bread, and pastries. Pre-orders via WhatsApp. Delivery within a city area.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Custom Cakes', 'Naming Ceremony Cakes', 'Wedding Cakes'. Minimum order in NGN. Delivery lead time. 'Order via WhatsApp'.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian celebration cake with Afrocentric decoration. Nigerian baker in white apron with finished product. Pastry display case with Nigerian baked goods.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: NAFDAC compliance for commercial food production. CAC Business Name registration.
nextAction: Research Nigerian bakery and custom cake website patterns and pre-order flow design
notes: M9 milestone. Custom/celebration cake pre-ordering is the primary business model.

africaFirstNotes: Event culture (owambe) is a defining feature of Nigerian social life
audienceSummary: Nigerians planning weddings, birthdays, corporate events, and owambes. They want to see menu options, event capacity, past event photos, and get a quote.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian catering is driven by owambe culture — elaborate events with jollof rice, pepper soup, small chops. The website must showcase past events, popular dishes, and make quote requests easy.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Small Chops', 'Jollof Rice', 'Pepper Soup', 'Owambe Specials'. Pricing per head in NGN. Minimum guest count. Quote request via WhatsApp.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian owambe food spread — jollof rice, puff-puff, chin-chin, small chops. Catering van with branded signage. Nigerian event setup with food stations.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: NAFDAC food handling compliance. Lagos State Environment and Health compliance (for Lagos operators). Catering event insurance.
nextAction: Research Nigerian event catering website patterns and quote request flow
notes: M9 milestone. Owambe event culture is the central content frame.

audienceSummary: Nigerian professionals and middle-class customers seeking relaxation and wellness services. Lagos / Abuja primary market.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian spas serve urban professional market. Services: Swedish massage, hot stone, facials, body scrubs. Gift vouchers common for corporate gifting.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Couples Massage', 'Corporate Packages', 'Gift Vouchers'. Price in NGN per session. 'Book a Session via WhatsApp'. Nigerian wellness language.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian client relaxing during massage treatment. Spa interior with candles and Nigerian décor. Wellness product display.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: State Environmental Health licence. Business Name registration.
nextAction: Research Nigerian spa and wellness website patterns and booking flow design
notes: M10 milestone. Corporate gifting and couples packages are common Nigerian spa market differentiators.

audienceSummary: Urban Nigerian professionals and fitness enthusiasts seeking gym membership. They want to see equipment, class schedule, membership fees in NGN, and location.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian gyms serve the growing urban fitness market. Monthly membership fees in NGN. Group classes (aerobics, Zumba, yoga) are popular. Personal trainer packages.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Monthly Membership', 'Day Pass', 'Personal Training', 'Group Classes'. Price in NGN per month. Reference Nigerian fitness culture and body goals language.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian gym members working out — diverse ages and body types. Gym floor with modern equipment. Personal trainer with Nigerian client.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: State Environmental Health registration. Fitness instructor certifications.
nextAction: Research Nigerian gym and fitness centre website patterns and membership sign-up flow
notes: M10 milestone. Class schedule and membership pricing are primary content areas.

nicheName: Real Estate Agency / Property Listings Site
audienceSummary: Nigerians searching for property to buy or rent. Lagos, Abuja, PH markets. They want price in NGN, location by area, property photos, and agent contact.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian real estate is a high-value, high-trust sector. Land banking fraud is a real concern — agent credibility (years in business, NIESV registration, past sales) is critical.
contentLocalizationNotes: List properties by Nigerian areas: Lekki, VI, Ikoyi, Ajah, Gwarinpa, Maitama, GRA. Price in NGN (millions). 'Inspect Property via WhatsApp'. 'Verified Agent'.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian property exterior — Lagos duplex, Abuja estate, GRA bungalow. Agent showing Nigerian couple around property. Land title document close-up.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: NIESV (Nigerian Institution of Estate Surveyors and Valuers) registration. CAC registration. LASRESA licence for Lagos agents.
nextAction: Research Nigerian real estate agency website patterns and property listing trust signals
notes: M9 milestone. Property fraud awareness makes agent credibility signals critical.

africaFirstNotes: Intra-Africa tourism (Lagos-Accra, Lagos-Nairobi) is growing; Dubai and Turkey are top international destinations for Nigerians
audienceSummary: Nigerians planning holidays, honeymoons, or business trips. They want package deals, visa assistance, and price in NGN.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian travel agents sell flight-plus-hotel packages, visa processing, and group tours. Dubai, Turkey, UK, and USA are top destinations. Intra-Africa tourism is growing. IATA accreditation is a trust signal.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature popular Nigerian travel routes. Price in NGN. 'Visa Assistance Available'. 'Book Your Trip via WhatsApp'. IATA accreditation badge.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian traveller at international airport. Popular destination imagery (Dubai, Turkey, Zanzibar, South Africa) with Nigerian context. Visa and passport imagery.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: IATA accreditation. NITP (Nigerian Institute of Tourism Professionals) membership. CAC registration.
nextAction: Research Nigerian travel agent website patterns and package deal presentation
notes: M10 milestone. IATA accreditation and visa processing are key differentiators.

nicheName: IT Support / Tech Service Provider Site
audienceSummary: Nigerian businesses and individuals needing IT support, computer repair, CCTV installation, or network setup.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian IT support ranges from one-person computer repairers in Computer Village (Ikeja) to multi-person business IT support firms. Generator-powered offices mean UPS and power management is a common service.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'CCTV Installation', 'Network Setup', 'Computer Repair', 'UPS/Inverter Installation'. Flat-rate prices in NGN. 'Remote Support Available'. Office IT contract packages.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian technician repairing laptop at Ikeja Computer Village. Server rack installation in Nigerian office. CCTV camera on Nigerian building.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: NITDA certification. Cisco/Microsoft certifications as trust signals. CAC registration.
nextAction: Research Nigerian IT support provider website patterns and service listing
notes: M9 milestone. Individual entity type. Computer Village (Ikeja, Lagos) is the hub of this vertical in Nigeria.

audienceSummary: Nigerian homeowners and landlords needing plumbing, electrical, tiling, painting, or general maintenance work. They find tradespeople via WhatsApp referrals and Google.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian handymen and tradespeople work across Lagos, Abuja, and all major cities. Referral trust is paramount — testimonials from named clients matter greatly.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature services: 'Plumbing', 'Electrical', 'Tiling', 'Painting', 'False Ceiling'. Price 'per job' or 'call-out fee' in NGN. 'Call/WhatsApp for a quote'.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian plumber fixing pipes. Nigerian electrician wiring a socket. Tiler laying Nigerian tiles in home. Before-and-after photos.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: COREN (Council for the Regulation of Engineering in Nigeria) for licensed engineers. Relevant professional trade certificate.
nextAction: Research Nigerian handyman and home services website patterns and service booking design
notes: M9 milestone. Individual entity type. WhatsApp referral and ratings are the trust foundation.

audienceSummary: Nigerian businesses and individuals needing tax filing, FIRS compliance, VAT registration, and financial planning. SMEs and sole traders are the primary audience.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian tax consultants navigate FIRS, LIRS, and state Internal Revenue Services. ICAN membership is the primary professional trust signal. Filing deadlines and VAT compliance are common service areas.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'FIRS Compliance', 'VAT Registration', 'Annual Returns', 'Tax Clearance Certificate'. Price per filing or retainer in NGN. ICAN membership number prominent.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian accountant at desk with tax documents. FIRS office documents. Calculator with NGN currency symbol.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: ICAN (Institute of Chartered Accountants of Nigeria) membership. CITN (Chartered Institute of Taxation of Nigeria) membership. FIRS accreditation.
nextAction: Research Nigerian tax consultant website patterns and FIRS/ICAN compliance display
notes: M10 milestone. Individual entity type. ICAN/CITN credentials are the primary trust signals.

africaFirstNotes: Ankara/Kente fabric, Aso-Ebi culture, and Nigerian fashion designers have global reach — Deola Sagoe, Emmy Kasbit, Orange Culture
audienceSummary: Nigerian fashion buyers, Aso-Ebi shoppers, and diaspora customers. They want to see the collection, fabric quality, custom order options, and WhatsApp ordering.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian fashion brands range from Ankara-fabric designers to contemporary Afrocentric labels. Aso-Ebi wholesale and custom orders are major revenue streams. Instagram is primary discovery but website handles volume orders.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Ankara Collections', 'Aso-Ebi Packages', 'Custom Orders', 'Ready-to-Wear'. Price in NGN per yard or per piece. 'Order via WhatsApp'. Fabric stories.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian model in Ankara print outfit. Aso-Ebi bridal party in matching fabrics. Nigerian fashion designer at work. Lagos fashion week aesthetic.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: CAC Business Name registration. Copyright of original designs. Customs/import documentation for fabric sourcing.
nextAction: Research Nigerian fashion brand website patterns and Aso-Ebi ordering flow
notes: M9 milestone. Aso-Ebi wholesale and custom order management are the primary Pillar 2 features.

audienceSummary: Nigerians booking photographers for weddings, corporate events, naming ceremonies, and portraits.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian photography studios are primarily event-driven — weddings, owambes, corporate headshots, naming ceremonies. Portfolio is the primary trust signal. WhatsApp booking is standard.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Wedding Photography', 'Corporate Headshots', 'Naming Ceremony', 'Owambe Coverage'. Price per event in NGN. Portfolio gallery by event type.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian wedding photography — couple in Yoruba/Igbo/Hausa traditional attire. Corporate headshot in Nigerian office. Naming ceremony celebration.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: No specific national licence. Professional photography association (NUP) membership optional.
blockers: Verify canonical slug: CSV has 'photography' (vtx_photography) but migration 0037 uses 'photography-studio'. Confirm correct slug before implementing.
nextAction: Verify slug: check if vertical slug should be 'photography' or 'photography-studio' in D1
notes: M9 milestone. Slug ambiguity: vtx_photography in CSV but migration 0037 references photography-studio. Check D1 before implementing.

nicheName: Music Studio / Recording Artist Profile Site
africaFirstNotes: Afrobeats is Nigeria's primary cultural export — music studio websites serve a global audience
audienceSummary: Music artists seeking studio time, labels scouting talent, fans following an artist, and event promoters booking for shows.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian music studios are in the centre of the global Afrobeats movement. Studio booking is WhatsApp-driven. Artist profile sites showcase discography, bookings, and press kit.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Book Studio Session', 'Production Credits', 'Discography', 'Booking for Events'. Studio rates in NGN per hour. Artist's streaming links (Spotify, Apple Music, Audiomack, Boomplay).
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian artist in recording studio. Producer at console with Nigerian artist. Album artwork in Afrobeats aesthetic.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: COSON (Copyright Society of Nigeria) registration. AMS (Association of Music Producers) membership for studios.
nextAction: Research Nigerian music studio and artist profile website patterns and streaming integration
notes: M9 milestone. Dual use: music studio (B2B session booking) and artist profile (B2C fan engagement).

audienceSummary: Nigerians booking a venue for weddings, birthday parties, corporate events, and naming ceremonies. They want capacity, photos, catering options, and availability calendar.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian event halls are booked months in advance for owambe season. Key info: capacity (number of guests), A/C, generator, kitchen, decorating allowed, price per day in NGN.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Capacity: 200-500 guests', '24-hour Generator', 'In-house Catering Optional', 'Décor Allowed'. Price per event day in NGN. 'Check Availability via WhatsApp'.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian event hall set for a wedding reception. Hall interior with Nigerian table settings. Exterior shot with Nigerian event crowd outside.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: State fire service clearance. Lagos State Urban Development certificate for large venues. CAC registration.
nextAction: Research Nigerian event hall website patterns and availability calendar design
notes: M9 milestone. Place entity type. Booking calendar and capacity display are critical features.

pillar2EligibilitySource: migration 0037_verticals_primary_pillars.sql — commerce branding group (law-firm slug confirmed in CSV vtx_law_firm)
audienceSummary: Nigerians and businesses seeking legal representation — property law, corporate law, family law, criminal defence. They want to verify NBA membership and understand practice areas.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian law firms range from sole practitioners to corporate-law partnerships. NBA membership and Supreme Court enrollment are primary trust signals. Nigerians seek lawyers via referral and Google.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Practice Areas' (Commercial Law, Property Law, Employment Law, Criminal Law). NBA membership number. 'Book a Consultation' via WhatsApp. Nigerian court system references.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian lawyer in wig and gown at court. Law firm reception with Nigerian statute books. Professional headshot of Nigerian lawyer.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: NBA (Nigerian Bar Association) membership. Supreme Court of Nigeria enrolment. CAC registration for firms. SANship for Senior Advocates.
nextAction: Research Nigerian law firm website patterns and NBA trust signal display
notes: M9 milestone. NBA membership is the primary trust signal. SAN (Senior Advocate of Nigeria) designation is a premium signal.

nicheName: Thrift / Ajo / Esusu Group Member Portal
africaFirstNotes: Ajo (Yoruba), Esusu (Yoruba/Igbo), Adashe (Hausa), Osusu (Igbo) — savings circle model found across all Nigerian ethnic groups and West Africa broadly
audienceSummary: Ajo/esusu group members seeking to track contributions, check payout schedule, and communicate with the group coordinator.
businessContextSummary: Ajo is Nigeria's informal financial backbone. Groups of 10-50 members contribute fixed amounts weekly/monthly; each member collects in rotation. The Pillar 2 portal is a members-only dashboard showing contribution history and next payout.
contentLocalizationNotes: Use 'Ajo', 'Esusu', 'Contribution Cycle', 'Payout Date'. All amounts in NGN. Reference oral trust tradition and WhatsApp group coordination.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian women's savings circle meeting. Market women collecting ajo contributions. Community meeting with contribution ledger.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: No formal Nigerian regulation for informal savings groups. CAC incorporation if formalising as cooperative.
nextAction: Research ajo/esusu digital management patterns and member portal design
notes: M9 milestone. This is a members-only portal, not a public consumer website. Deeply Nigeria-specific model.

audienceSummary: Nigerians seeking motor insurance, health insurance, or life insurance. They want NAICOM-licensed agent verification and policy comparison.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian insurance agents are regulated by NAICOM. Motor insurance (third-party) is compulsory in Nigeria. Health Insurance (NHIS) and life insurance are growing. Trust is a major barrier in the sector.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Third-Party Motor Insurance', 'Health Insurance', 'NAICOM Licensed Agent'. Price ranges in NGN. 'Get a Quote via WhatsApp'. Policy comparison table.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian insurance agent with client reviewing policy documents. Car insurance certificate with Nigerian number plate reference.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: NAICOM (National Insurance Commission) licence number. Insurer partnership logos.
nextAction: Research Nigerian insurance agent website patterns and NAICOM trust signal display
notes: M10 milestone. Individual entity type. NAICOM licence is the primary trust signal.

audienceSummary: Nigerians seeking to obtain a driver's licence or improve driving skills. They want FRSC-accredited school confirmation, course fees in NGN, and schedule.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian driving schools must be FRSC-accredited. The course leads to the Nigerian Driver's Licence issued by FRSC. Course duration (4-8 weeks), fees in NGN, and FRSC accreditation are the trust signals.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'FRSC Accredited', 'Automatic and Manual Transmission', 'Theory + Practical', course fee in NGN. Schedule by LGA. 'Register via WhatsApp'.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian learner driver at wheel with instructor. FRSC accreditation certificate on wall. Driving school vehicle with Nigerian number plate.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: FRSC accreditation is MANDATORY and must be displayed prominently. State FRSC office registration.
nextAction: Research Nigerian driving school website patterns and FRSC accreditation display
notes: M9 milestone. FRSC accreditation is the essential trust signal and a legal requirement.

pillar2EligibilitySource: migration 0037_verticals_primary_pillars.sql — commerce branding group (slug 'vocational' maps to CSV 'training-institute')
audienceSummary: Nigerian youths and adults seeking vocational skills — ICT, fashion design, catering, carpentry, electrical. They want to see courses, fees, certification, and location.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian vocational/technical institutes are critical to youth employment. NABTEB and NBTE certification adds credibility. Course fees in NGN. Placement support is a differentiator.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Accredited by NABTEB', 'Certified Courses', course fees in NGN per term. 'Apply Now via WhatsApp'. Placement rates and alumni stories.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian students learning skills — sewing machines, welding equipment, computers. Nigerian instructor teaching class. Certificate presentation.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: NABTEB accreditation. NBTE (National Board for Technical Education) approval. CAC registration.
blockers: Slug mismatch: migration 0037 uses 'vocational' but CSV canonical slug is 'training-institute'. Remediation migration needed.
nextAction: Confirm slug remediation migration is applied, then begin research
notes: M9 milestone. Slug mismatch flag. NABTEB accreditation is key trust signal.

audienceSummary: Nigerian parents seeking tutors for their children — WAEC/NECO preparation, JAMB coaching, subject-specific help.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian private tutoring is driven by exam pressure (WAEC, NECO, JAMB, Common Entrance). Individual tutors or small lesson centres. Sessions in person at student home, teacher home, or lesson centre.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'WAEC/NECO Prep', 'JAMB Coaching', 'Common Entrance', subjects offered. Rate per hour in NGN. 'Contact via WhatsApp to book a session'.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian student studying with textbooks. Tutor explaining mathematics to Nigerian secondary school student. Lesson centre with students.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: NCE (Nigeria Certificate in Education) or university degree as tutor qualification. WAEC/NECO pass rate claims must be verifiable.
nextAction: Research Nigerian private tutoring website patterns and WAEC/JAMB exam prep content
notes: M9 milestone. Individual entity type. Exam success rates are the primary trust signal.

africaFirstNotes: Onitsha Main Market is the largest open market in Africa — Pillar 2 here serves market associations and wholesale operators
audienceSummary: Nigerian retailers and traders sourcing goods wholesale — electronics (Alaba), automotive parts (Ladipo), textiles (Onitsha), food (Mile 12). B2B price discovery.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian wholesale markets operate by stall. The 'website' is a market directory — listing stalls, goods available, and price ranges. Market association governance is central.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature market name prominently ('Alaba International', 'Onitsha Main Market'). Stall numbers. Product categories. 'Call/WhatsApp Stall Owner'. Prices 'from ₦X,000 per unit'.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian open market with stalls and goods. Alaba market electronics. Onitsha textile market with Ankara fabric bales. Traders and customers.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: Market Association registration. State government market authority recognition. CAC for market association.
nextAction: Research Nigerian wholesale market directory patterns and B2B price discovery design
notes: M9 milestone. Place entity type. This is a B2B market directory, not B2C retail.

audienceSummary: Nigerian importers, manufacturers, and FMCG distributors seeking warehousing and storage solutions.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian warehouses serve importers and FMCG companies. Key information: location (by axis/expressway), storage capacity (square metres/pallet positions), temperature control, security.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Lagos-Ibadan Expressway', 'Apapa Port Adjacent', capacity in square metres, storage rates in NGN per month. 'Request Quote via WhatsApp'.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian warehouse interior with shelving and forklifts. Goods being palletised. Port-adjacent warehouse exterior.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: CAC registration. Fire service compliance. NAFDAC cold-chain certification for food storage.
nextAction: Research Nigerian warehouse operator website patterns and capacity/rate display
notes: M10 milestone. B2B service. Inventory slot management is the core operational feature.

pillar2EligibilitySource: migration 0037_verticals_primary_pillars.sql — commerce branding group (slug 'dental' maps to CSV 'dental-clinic')
audienceSummary: Nigerians seeking dental consultation, teeth cleaning, or orthodontic treatment. They want MDCN/NDHRA-registered dentist verification and price range.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian dental clinics serve urban middle-class. MDCN registration and NDHRA (Nigerian Dental and Health Related Academia) are trust signals. Services: scaling/polishing, extractions, orthodontics.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Scaling & Polishing', 'Extraction', 'Dentures', 'Braces/Orthodontics'. Prices in NGN per service. 'Book via WhatsApp'. MDCN registration number.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian dentist examining patient in chair. Modern dental equipment. Dentist in scrubs with mask.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: MDCN registration. NDHRA membership. CAC registration.
blockers: Slug mismatch: migration 0037 uses 'dental' but CSV canonical slug is 'dental-clinic'. Remediation migration needed before this template can be SHIPPED.
nextAction: Confirm slug remediation migration; template can be built now but SHIPPED only after migration confirmed
notes: M10 milestone. Slug mismatch flag. MDCN registration is the primary trust signal.

pillar2EligibilitySource: migration 0037_verticals_primary_pillars.sql — commerce branding group (slug 'vet' maps to CSV 'vet-clinic')
audienceSummary: Nigerian pet owners seeking veterinary care, vaccinations, and grooming for dogs, cats, and birds.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian pet ownership is growing rapidly in urban areas. Vet clinics provide consultations, vaccinations (rabies, parvo), and pet products. VCNR registration is the trust signal.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Dog/Cat Vaccination', 'Deworming', 'Pet Surgery', 'Pet Shop'. Prices in NGN per service. 'Book Appointment via WhatsApp'. Emergency vet contact.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian vet examining dog or cat. Pet owner with pet at Nigerian vet clinic. Puppies and kittens in clean pet shop.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: VCNR (Veterinary Council of Nigeria Registration). FMoA (Federal Ministry of Agriculture) import licences for pet drugs.
blockers: Slug mismatch: migration 0037 uses 'vet' but CSV canonical slug is 'vet-clinic'. Remediation migration needed.
nextAction: Confirm slug remediation migration; template can be built now but SHIPPED only after migration confirmed
notes: M10 milestone. Slug mismatch flag. VCNR registration is the primary trust signal.

nicheName: Crèche / Day Care Centre Enrollment Site
audienceSummary: Nigerian working parents seeking safe, registered day care for children aged 0-5. They want to verify state registration, see facilities, and check fees.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian crèches and day care centres cater to working parents in urban areas. State SUBEB or Ministry of Education registration is required. Safety and supervision ratio are trust signals.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'State Registered', 'Ages 0-5', 'Breakfast & Lunch Included', fees per month in NGN. NDPR consent on enrollment form. 'Schedule a Visit via WhatsApp'.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian children in colourful crèche environment. Nigerian early childhood teacher reading to toddlers. Safe play area with Nigerian children.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: SUBEB registration. State Ministry of Education approval. Child protection policies. NDPR applies — children's data requires parental consent.
nextAction: Research Nigerian crèche and day care website patterns and enrollment trust signals
notes: M10 milestone. P13 governance: minor data requires L3 HITL. NDPR parental consent is mandatory for any child data.

nicheName: Mobile Money / POS Agent Service Site
pillar2EligibilitySource: migration 0037_verticals_primary_pillars.sql — commerce branding group (slug 'mobile-money' maps to CSV 'mobile-money-agent')
africaFirstNotes: Mobile money agent banking model dominant across sub-Saharan Africa (M-Pesa Kenya, MTN Momo, OPay, PalmPay)
audienceSummary: Nigerians looking for a nearby POS agent for cash withdrawal, transfer, or bill payment. Location-based search is primary discovery method.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian POS agents provide cash-out, transfer, and bill payment services for the unbanked and underbanked. CBN licencing of agent banking is through parent banks (OPay, PalmPay, First Bank, GTBank). Float management is the core operational challenge.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Cash Withdrawal', 'Bank Transfer', 'Bill Payment', 'Airtime Top-Up'. Limits and charges in NGN. Opening hours. 'Call/WhatsApp for availability'.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian POS agent with POS terminal on table. Queue of customers at POS point. Signage board showing services and rates.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: CBN agent banking licence (via parent institution). Parent bank affiliation (OPay, PalmPay, First Bank, etc.) logo display.
blockers: Slug mismatch: migration 0037 uses 'mobile-money' but CSV canonical slug is 'mobile-money-agent'. Remediation migration needed.
nextAction: Confirm slug remediation migration; template can be built now but SHIPPED only after migration confirmed
notes: M9 milestone. Slug mismatch flag. CBN agent banking compliance required. Different from POS business management — this is agent banking not inventory management.

nicheName: Bureau de Change / FX Dealer Site
pillar2EligibilitySource: migration 0037_verticals_primary_pillars.sql — commerce branding group (slug 'bdc' maps to CSV 'bureau-de-change')
audienceSummary: Nigerians and businesses needing to buy or sell USD, GBP, EUR, or other currencies. They want current exchange rates, CBN licence verification, and location.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian BDCs are CBN-licensed to buy and sell foreign currency. They compete with parallel market rates. CBN licence number is the most critical trust signal and a legal requirement.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'CBN Licensed BDC', live or indicative exchange rates (USD/NGN, GBP/NGN, EUR/NGN). Opening hours. 'WhatsApp for today's rate'. KYC requirements for large transactions.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian BDC operator at counter with currency display board. Naira notes and foreign currency. CBN licence certificate visible.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: CBN BDC licence number (MANDATORY — must display prominently). NFIU AML compliance declaration. Daily transaction limit notice.
blockers: Slug mismatch: migration 0037 uses 'bdc' but CSV canonical slug is 'bureau-de-change'. Remediation migration needed.
nextAction: Confirm slug remediation migration; template can be built now but SHIPPED only after migration confirmed
notes: M10 milestone. Slug mismatch flag. CBN BDC licence is a legal requirement for operation and must display on the site.

audienceSummary: Nigerian businesses and individuals seeking to acquire assets (generator, vehicle, equipment) on credit/hire purchase. They want to understand the terms, interest rate, and eligibility.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian hire purchase companies finance generators, vehicles, solar panels, and commercial equipment. CAC registration and CBN-adjacent compliance apply. Monthly repayment in NGN is the primary product feature.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Generator Finance', 'Vehicle Finance', 'Solar Panel Finance'. Monthly repayment in NGN. Interest rate per annum (transparent). 'Apply via WhatsApp'.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian business owner receiving equipment key. Generator delivery to Nigerian home. Vehicle fleet at hire purchase company lot.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: CAC registration. CBN-regulated lending compliance declaration. FIRS TIN.
nextAction: Research Nigerian hire purchase company website patterns and credit product presentation
notes: M11 milestone. Financial product — lending compliance declaration required on site.

nicheName: Community Hall / Civic Space Booking Site
audienceSummary: Nigerian community organisers, religious groups, and social event planners seeking affordable local hall hire.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian community halls serve as wedding venues, church event spaces, and town meeting halls. Managed by community development associations, LGAs, or churches. Booking is often manual and informal.
contentLocalizationNotes: Feature 'Community Development Association', capacity, hall equipment (chairs, tables, stage, PA system). Rate per event in NGN. 'Check Availability via WhatsApp'.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Nigerian community hall set for an event. Town hall meeting in session. Exterior of community hall with Nigerian signage.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: LGA community development authority registration. Fire safety certificate.
nextAction: Research Nigerian community hall booking website patterns
notes: M11 milestone. Place entity type. Low-cost, civic-focused template.
